title: Mustardy Monsoon Dutch Speck Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew originating from the Netherlands, featuring speck and a mustardy lacquer glaze, perfect for a rainy day. This dish is Jain-friendly and cooked to perfection in a kazan cauldron.
instructions: Heat the kazan cauldron over medium heat and add the diced speck to cook until browned.
Add the chopped onion to the cauldron and cook until it is translucent.
Add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes to the cauldron, cooking for 5 minutes.
Stir in the whole grain mustard and lacquer glaze, cooking for an additional 2 minutes.
Pour in the low sodium vegetable broth, adding the dried thyme and fresh bay leaves to the cauldron.
Season the stew with salt and black pepper to taste.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Remove the bay leaves from the cauldron and serve the stew hot.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
Serve with crusty bread or over mashed potatoes for a filling meal.
